posts.caption:
@zaralarsson is flooding all our feeds. Be it the soaring success of her fifth studio album #MidnightSun or the way in which #PinkPantheress's 'Stateside', featuring Larsson, took off at the Winter Olympics after #AlysaLiu skated to their track on the ice, there's been an undeniable, star-like quality to Larsson, who has been blazing through her world tour stops as of late.
And she's not about to let the dust settle—as she meets us on the cover of @voguesingapore's 'Embody' issue as one of two cover stars. "Performing live has been quite emotional and very rewarding. You can look at numbers online, but when a room gets bigger because more people want to come and dance and sing with you, that's something different."
In the link in bio, the electrifying pop star shares how her sound has always been inflected by her musical foundations in Sweden, connecting with her fans, and why she's never been able to imagine not doing music.
The #VogueSingapore June 'Embody' issue is now out on newsstands.
Editor-in-chief: @monkiepoo
Photographer: @benjaminvnuk
Videographer: @philip.unrmbrd

posts.caption: Luxury retail spaces are never just storefronts: they sit as homes for the brand, around which a maison's language centres itself. #Hermès has taken this to the next level with their new 166 New Bond Street location—inscribing the Hermès experience and prestige into the six-townhouse maison. More than a decade in the making, it is one of the most significant luxury retail openings in recent years; a transformation of one of Mayfair’s most storied addresses into a timeless expression of the French maison. As #VogueSingapore’s editor-in-chief @monkiepoo made the journey to London just for the occasion, read his reflections upon entering the newly-opened 166, now in the link in bio.

posts.caption: How do Vogue editors do summer-in-the-city style? Fashion market director Madeline Fass (@mfass) balances city elegance with summer nonchalance by mixing and matching staples. Shop her wish list filled with the season’s best finds at the link in our bio. Photo 1 and 4 by @theochoi_
posts.alt_text: Defined by clean lines, thoughtful materials, and an absence of unnecessary embellishment, the minimalist dress has become the quiet hero of the modern, well-curated wardrobe. Discover five effortless ways to wear one—plus a few extras to consider at the link in our bio.

posts.caption: Warm weather means there’s less to think about—no layers or complicated styling formulas. What could be easier on a sunny morning than slipping into a one-and-done dress and heading out for the day? Adding loafers to the mix. They bring an unexpected attitude to dresses—or, in Kendall Jenner’s case, an oversized button-up—with their gamine, preppy charm. Re-create—and shop—some of our favorite summer combinations at the link in bio.

: 👶❤️ Having children is one of life’s biggest decisions—and smart couples know it takes more than just love.

Because preparing for parenthood isn't about having all the answers—it's about growing together and making intentional choices. 🤍

posts.caption:
: ❤️ Marriage isn't just about grand gestures—it's the little "unhinged" things couples do that keep the romance alive. ✨
From random dance breaks in the kitchen to leaving cute notes, stealing fries, and laughing over inside jokes, sometimes the most unexpected habits become the strongest relationship rituals.
💍 Which of these "unhinged" marriage habits would you actually do with your partner? Or do you already have one? Tell us in the comments! 👇
📌 Save this post for your future spouse (or tag your partner for a hint 😉).
📲 Follow @weddingaffairofficial for real relationship insights & modern love inspiration 💎
#marriage #couplegoals❤ #relationshipgoals #marriedlife💍 #healthyrelationships

posts.alt_text:
A stage. A story. A Rajkumari. 

RAJKUMARI is back, MY BIGGEST INDIA TOUR YET. (If you don’t see your city don’t worry, more cities to be added). 
Tickets on Book My Show.

What is Rajkumari?

An evening of stories, poems dipped in music. This is not just a show. It’s a gentle rebellion. Against everything you were told to be.A space to see differently.
To live differently. Your way. A reminder of the power of your own perspective, of your “Nayab Nazariya”.

After touring in 6 countries, Rajkumari is back in India. Come for one of India’s biggest storytelling & Poetry show.

Tickets on @bookmyshowin , link in my bio🔗

posts.caption:
✨ Creator, Actor, PHENOMENON! ✨ On the June-July cover of Wedding Vows, we present the trailblazing @mostlysane, Prajakta Koli!
From building a YouTube empire to being the lead for Netflix Original Mismatched, from being part of Michelle Obama’s Day Time Emmy winning docu-series to being UNDP India's First Youth Climate Champion, her journey is nothing short of extraordinary.
With her debut romance novel set to launch this year, along with her first Amazon Prime Original Andhera, Prajakta’s journey is a testament to the power of dreams, hard work, and staying true to oneself. She continues to inspire millions with her authenticity, relatability, and unstoppable spirit.

posts.caption:
She doesn’t enter a room. She expands it.
Industrialist. Angel Investor. Former owner of the CBL Telugu team. Nandiniy Vijay @nandiniy_vijay has spent her career doing what most people only talk about — backing bold ideas before the market catches up. From the badminton courts to boardrooms across industries, she has moved with the conviction of someone who has never once waited for permission to lead.
At WV Connect, she brought that same energy. The kind that makes everyone around her sharper. Her instinct for spotting what’s next, her clarity on what actually works, and her refusal to settle for ordinary made our boardroom a genuinely better place to be.
When we talked about bringing Weddings and Films together, she saw it immediately. Not just as a creative vision but as a cultural moment whose time had arrived.
The boardroom just got more powerful. And Hyderabad is about to feel it. 🎬🧡
WV Connect Season 4 | Sep 1, 2 & 3, 2026 | Arth Resorts & Spa, Hyderabad #LightsCameraShaadi #WVConnect2026 #HyderabadZaroorAana
posts.alt_text:
Most people in a room talk strategy. Sanjeev @sanjeev_korada lives it.

CEO of Glosel, Founder of PathScript @pathscript , three decades of navigating global markets and building businesses that actually work. He has sat across the table from MNCs, SMEs, and everyone in between, and helped them find their way through the fog. That’s not a skill you learn. That’s a lens you develop over a lifetime.

What he brought to the WV Connect boardroom wasn’t just business acumen. It was clarity. When the ideas were flying and the energy was everywhere, he was the one who helped us see the path forward. Grounded. Sharp. Always asking the right questions.

An industry full of creativity needed someone who understood structure. He is exactly that.

When we said Weddings meets Films this season, he saw it not just as a theme but as a business move whose time had come.

The strategist the boardroom needed. 🎬🧡

Venue Partner : @arthresorts
An Experience by @weddingvows.in
In Strategic Alliance with @designby_raja

WV Connect Season 4 | Sep 1, 2 & 3, 2026 | Arth Resorts & Spa, Hyderabad #LightsCameraShaadi #WVConnect2026 #HyderabadZaroorAana

posts.caption:
Before there was a stage, there was someone who believed there should be one.
Thilak Venkatasamy @thilakmaiam is that someone for WV Connect. Founder and CEO of Barottas, Shero Home Foods, Angel Investor, and the quiet force behind this entire brainchild. The kind of person who sees what something can become long before anyone else can see what it already is.
He didn’t just invest. He mentored. Pushed. Stayed in the room when the conversations got hard and kept the compass pointing in the right direction. WV Connect exists in the form it does today because of his belief in it.
In the boardroom he’s the one who asks the question nobody else thought to ask. And somehow that question always unlocks everything.
When Weddings met Films this season, it was that same instinct at work. See the possibility. Back it completely.
The maverick. The mentor. The reason. 🎬🧡
WV Connect Season 4 | Sep 1, 2 & 3, 2026 | Arth Resorts & Spa, Hyderabad
Venue Partner : @arthresorts
An Experience by @weddingvows.in
In Strategic Alliance with @designby_raja
#LightsCameraShaadi #WVConnect2026 #HyderabadZaroorAana

posts.caption:
@camimendes is having one of the biggest years in her career so far. A major summer blockbuster, the second feature film from her production company, and wedding planning are all on the roster for 2026. This month, Mendes steps into the sprawling world of Masters of the Universe as the sword-slinging Teela alongside Nicholas Galitzine, Idris Elba, and Jared Leto.
The project is a stretch from her days as Veronica Lodge in #Riverdale, which catapulted her into mainstream recognition nearly a decade ago. posts.alt_text:
There would be no Pope Heyward without @jonathandavissofficial. Much like his smart, careful, deeply moral Outer Banks character, Daviss knows what it feels like to carry the pressure of proving your worth and refusing to let yourself or anyone else down. “What really stood out to me about Pope in season 1 was this idea of expectations and pressure that gets put on a lot of young Black teens and teens in general,” he says. “There’s this pressure to succeed, and it was something I truly have been through.”

Daviss still thinks back to his early audition days before landing the life-changing role on OBX, when the constant need to prove himself as a young actor felt emotionally exhausting. His mindset then? “You only lose when you quit.”

Head to the link in our bio for the full download in our Now Playing Issue presented by @bvlgari, from what to expect from the fifth and final season of OBX to his upcoming portrayal of a young Snoop Dogg.

posts.caption:
@victoriamonet is trading her claws for butterfly wings. The three-time Grammy winner is the definition of a multi-hyphenate: a culinary school graduate, a mother, and, of course, a singer-songwriter with a nearly two-decade-spanning catalog to her name. Now, using her 37th birthday as a launching pad, Monét has cleared her mental space and scrubbed her socials to prepare for her next chapter—an album she’s dubbed her very own Thriller, a sonically surprising, genre-defying project that marks a softer evolution. “The jaguar exists within me always, but I’m feeling like a softer version of myself now,” she says.
After all, Monét’s critically acclaimed debut studio album Jaguar II was the ultimate learning lesson. “Once you have an accolade like that, people often look for a reason to discredit why you got it,” she explains. “I think—pending a Grammy happens again—I would do the opposite and bunker down in the studio.”
Step inside Monét’s world in our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
Rei Kawakubo’s spring show for @commedesgarcons Homme Plus felt like a bookend to her nearly heartbreaking fall 2025 men’s effort, titled “To Hell With War.”⁠
⁠
Here it was “If the War Were to End,” and the spectacle brimmed with hope, vivid color and positive vibes.⁠
⁠
A year-and-a-half ago, her young models wore army boots with the toe box upturned at a 90-degree angle, a footwear style that suggested war is a dead end.⁠
⁠
Here, the boots had morphed into poulaines, their long, pointy toes curled up whimsically. Likewise, camouflage prints were blown up in size and recolored in psychedelic hues, bringing lava lamps to mind. Army helmets had been replaced by calico sun hats or bucket styles.⁠
⁠
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: Miles Socha⁠
Photos: @giovanni_giannoni_photo/WWD
posts.alt_text:
It’s a big year for @Hermès, and a transitional one, too. Earlier this year the brand bid farewell to its longtime men’s artistic director, Véronique Nichanian, after 37 years and has welcomed her successor, British designer Grace Wales Bonner, who will unveil her first collection for the brand in January.

So it’s no surprise that Hermès kept its presentation low-key with a showroom presentation, and around 40 looks, including breezy shirts, perforated leather jackets and featherweight knits, on display.

The in-house design team, which had worked for years under Nichanian, stayed the course with a sophisticated collection full of fine tailoring details, delicate leatherwork and fresh takes on silk. They worked in a palette of muted tones, including pewter, faded blue, and ice white, alongside delicious shades of coffee, caramel and chocolate.

Read the full collection review at WWD.com — #linkinbio 

Report: @samanthavconti 
Video: @hey_imlara

posts.caption:
While Sarah Burton debuted her first @Givenchy presentation during men’s fashion week in Paris on Thursday, it’s hardly her first effort.
She’s been quietly overseeing men’s collections since being named creative director of the heritage house in late 2024, and menswear tailoring, one of her passions, has already informed her womenswear collections.
“The two collections talk to each other,” she said during a preview Tuesday evening, where looks from her spring 2027 menswear collection were displayed on mannequins amid sculptures by acclaimed English artist Rachel Whiteread.
“When I arrived, I put the design studios next to each other, so that the women’s and men’s teams talk to each other,” she said. “It’s almost a constant conversation that happens between the two studios, and sometimes an idea originates in men’s and then comes over to women’s and vice versa.”
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @milessocha

posts.caption:
Grace @WalesBonner described her collection as a “study in refinement and purity,” as she took inspiration from studio portraiture as well as documentary photography. She wanted to look at different characters and modes of self-expression, which is why there were some lovely contrasts, and quirky details.
Fabrics ranged from washed velvet, cotton and linen with a worn-in feel to a tougher python-printed leather. A stone-colored chore jacket was soft and slouchy, and came paired with matching workwear trousers with loops for tools and little rivets.
Knits ranged from the ribbed, striped and sporty to the grandmotherly, as in a taupe twin set with burgundy rosettes embroidered around the collar. Polo styles had a polished 1950s feel, and came in punchy color combinations — cobalt and black, cream and burgundy, which Wales Bonner teamed with drawstring shorts.
Read the full collection review at WWD.com - #linkinbio
Report: @samanthavconti
